title: The deposits package
text: deposits package uses R6 client interface individual deposition services. following sub-section explains properties deposits client unfamiliar R6 objects.

title: The deposits package
text: R6 package used construct deposits clients allows structured class objects R. objects include elements (variables) methods, R generally functions. new client can constructed new operator, deposits requires specifying service client constructed: Additional functions called similar way, using notation, cli$deposit_function(). deposits package constructed function calls constructed way “automatically” update object , generally need assigned return value. example, function deposits_list() updates list deposits associated service. conventional R packages, calling function require assigning return value like : R6 objects , however, always updated internally, client , cli, already include updated list deposits without need assigning return value cli_updated. , rather line, deposits functions may called simply , (single exception deposit_download_file() function, returns path locally downloaded file, always assigned return value.)
code: cli <- depositsClient$new (service = "figshare") cli_updated <- cli$deposits_list () cli$deposits_list ()

title: The deposits package
text: empty client can constructed naming desired service. additional sandbox parameter constructs client zenodo sandbox environment intended testing API. Actual use zenodo API can enabled default sandbox = FALSE. Client construction requires personal access authentication tokens deposits services stored local environment variables, described main README document. Authentication tokens checked new clients constructed, $new() function succeed valid tokens. also described README, main functions deposits client prefixed either deposit_ deposits_, seen result ls(cli): client constructed mostly empty, nevertheless demonstrates two primary fields elements deposits client: hostdata holding metadata “host” external deposits service particular deposit; metadata holding consistently structured representation key components hostdata. hostdata structures generally lists, differ different services, whereas metadata structures remain consistent services, allow data transformed one format another, , future functionality, allow data transferred different services. elements represent “metadata” deposit, data referred “files”, can uploaded downloaded. Thus deposits begin metadata, actual data upload possible initial metadata specified uploaded.
code: cli <- depositsClient$new ("zenodo", sandbox = TRUE) cli #> <deposits client> #> deposits service : zenodo #>           sandbox: TRUE #>         url_base : https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/ #> Current deposits : <none> #> #>   hostdata : <none> #>   metadata : <none> ls (cli) #>  [1] "deposit_delete"        "deposit_download_file" "deposit_fill_metadata" #>  [4] "deposit_new"           "deposit_retrieve"      "deposit_update" #>  [7] "deposit_upload_file"   "deposits"              "deposits_list" #> [10] "headers"               "hostdata"              "id" #> [13] "initialize"            "metadata"              "print" #> [16] "sandbox"               "service"               "term_map" #> [19] "url_base"              "url_service"

title: The deposits package
text: new deposit initially constructed filling metadata field local representation metadata. hostdata field filled initial deposit metadata uploaded external service. two fields thus differ metadata local, locally-defined, representation metadata deposit, whereas hostdata always provided defined external service. best way understand distinction practical demonstration.

title: The deposits package
text: several ways defining metadata deposits entity, perhaps easiest simple list: new deposits client can filled metadata passing metadata parameter: summary produced calling print() (, equivalently, just typing cli console) says object now includes three metadata terms. can seen viewing cli$metadata: Metadata deposits objects stored DCEntry objects format provided atom4R package. metadata primarily intended internal use within deposits package, shouldn’t generally need manipulated directly users package (although certainly can , illustrated ).
code: metadata <- list (     title = "New Title",     abstract = "This is the abstract",     creator = list ("A. Person", "B. Person") ) cli <- depositsClient$new (service = "zenodo", sandbox = TRUE, metadata = metadata) print (cli) #> <deposits client> #>  deposits service : zenodo #>            sandbox: TRUE #>          url_base : https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/ #>  Current deposits : <none> #> #>     hostdata : <none> #>     metadata : 3 terms (see 'metadata' element for details) cli$metadata #> <DCEntry> #> ....|-- updated: 2022-05-23 14:13:42 #> ....|-- title <DCTitle> #> ........|-- value: New Title #> ....|-- abstract <DCAbstract> #> ........|-- value: This is the abstract #> ....|-- creator <DCCreator> #> ........|-- value: A. Person #> ....|-- creator <DCCreator> #> ........|-- value: B. Person>

title: The deposits package
text: Another convenient way specify metadata use deposits_metadata_template() funciton write local YAML representation metadata. function also accepts metadata parameter data specified list: metadata can directly edited using text file editor, modified results saved local file. name file can also passed metadata parameter new deposits client. following code thus produces results :
code: meta_file <- tempfile (pattern = "meta-", fileext = ".yaml") deposits_metadata_template (filename = meta_file, metadata = metadata) head (readLines (meta_file)) #> [1] "{"                                                                                                  #> [2] "  \"_comment1\": \"Fields starting with underscores will be ignored (and can safely be deleted)\"," #> [3] "  \"Abstract\": \"This is the abstract\","                                                          #> [4] "  \"AccessRights\": \"\","                                                                          #> [5] "  \"AccrualMethod\": \"\","                                                                         #> [6] "  \"AccrualPeriodicity\": \"\"," cli <- depositsClient$new (service = "zenodo", sandbox = TRUE, metadata = meta_file)

title: The deposits package
text: deposits client metadata can used initiate new deposit associated external service $deposit_new() function. confused $new() function creates new client. $deposit_new() function uses existing client create new deposit external service. Using client constructed sample metadata gives following result: client now lists one current deposit, additional fields URL “id” deposit, hostdata field 14 elements. Importantly, id field holds unique integer value used identify particular deposits external services, deposit_id parameter deposits client functions. following code demonstrates constructing new client metadata. differs initial client now lists one “current deposit”. can examine get associated “id” value: can retrieve metadata previously uploaded deposit_retrieve() function: local client holds identical information previous client immediately calling deposit_new() - , retrieve_deposit() filled local client metadata deposit.
code: cli$deposit_new () print (cli) #> <deposits client> #>  deposits service : zenodo #>            sandbox: TRUE #>          url_base : https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/ #>  Current deposits : 1 (see 'deposits' element for details) #> #>  url_deposit : https://sandbox.zenodo.org/deposit/1064327 #>   deposit id : 1064327 #>     hostdata : list with 14  elements #>     metadata : 3 terms (see 'metadata' element for details) cli <- depositsClient$new (service = "zenodo", sandbox = TRUE) print (cli) #> <deposits client> #>  deposits service : zenodo #>            sandbox: TRUE #>          url_base : https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/ #>  Current deposits : 1 (see 'deposits' element for details) #> #>   hostdata : <none> #>   metadata : <none> cli$deposits$id #> [1] 1064327 deposit_id <- cli$deposits$id cli$deposit_retrieve (deposit_id = deposit_id)

title: The deposits package
text: deposits clients thus far used construct upload metadata. main point deposit course store actual data arbitrary format alongside structured metadata. achieved deposit_upload_file() function, demonstrated following code uses deposit retrieved directly . Although print output cli object change uploading, details files contained hostdata$files element: Files can downloaded converse download_file function, demonstrated first removing local copy, downloading deposits service:
code: path <- file.path (tempdir (), "data.Rds") saveRDS (datasets::Orange, path) cli$deposit_upload_file (path = path) cli$hostdata$files #>                           checksum               filename filesize #> 1 22c61006458c44ed979bf3461e330693 data-18e115882cf14.Rds      506 #>                                     id #> 1 00cad89d-4084-4337-b96a-2f1e232b2b6c #>                                                                                     links.download #> 1 https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/files/e3f727dc-1dfd-4849-88cf-994f630105cf/data-18e115882cf14.Rds #>                                                                                              links.self #> 1 https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/deposit/depositions/1064327/files/00cad89d-4084-4337-b96a-2f1e232b2b6c file.remove (path) file <- cli$deposit_download_file (filename = "data.Rds", path = tempdir ()) identical (readRDS (file), datasets::Orange) #> [1] TRUE

title: Installation and Setup
text: package can installed enabling “ropensci” r-universe, using install.packages(): Alternatively, package can installed directly GitHub following command: package can loaded use :
code: options (repos = c (     ropensci = "https://ropensci.r-universe.dev",     CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org" )) install.packages ("deposits") remotes::install_github ("ropenscilabs/deposits") library (deposits)

title: Installation and Setup
text: services require users create account generate API (“Application Programming Interface”) tokens. Click following links generate tokens, also listed sequences menu items used reach token settings: zenodo/account/settings/applications/tokens/new zenodo-sandbox/account/settings/applications/tokens/new, figshare/account/applications. necessary create register applications services; package uses personal tokens . tokens need stored local environment variables names must include names respective services, defined “name” column returned deposits_service(), shown . can done following example: Alternatively, tokens can stored ~/.Renviron file, automatically loaded every R session.
code: Sys.setenv ("ZENODO_SANDBOX_TOKEN" = "<my-token")

A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere
title:
A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere
text: deposits R package universal client depositing accessing research data anywhere. Currently supported services zenodo figshare. Instructions installing setting package separate vignette. README gives brief overview package usage, detail introductory vignette.

title:
A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere
text: list data repositories currently supported accessible deposits_services() function: zenodo offers “sandbox” environment, offers ideal environment testing functionality package.
code: library (deposits) deposits_services () ##             name                           docs                    api_base_url ## 1         zenodo https://developers.zenodo.org/         https://zenodo.org/api/ ## 2 zenodo-sandbox https://developers.zenodo.org/ https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/ ## 3       figshare     https://docs.figshare.com/    https://api.figshare.com/v2/

A universal client for depositing and accessing research data

A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere
text: Metadata data describing deposit, “data” deposit , generally uploaded files described . Metadata include details authors, titles, file types, descriptions, many terms. can included deposits client several ways, either initial construction client, separate step applied existing client. One easiest ways specify metadata list named terms like following example: data can used construction new client passing metadata argument: Equivalently, can added existing client deposit_fill_metadata() function: Note R6 functions called directly client, object (cli) updated call. ways specifying entering metadata described introductory vignette.
code: metadata <- list (     title = "New Title",     abstract = "This is the abstract",     creator = list ("A. Person", "B. Person") ) cli <- depositsClient$new (     service = "zenodo",     sandbox = TRUE,     metadata = metadata ) cli #> <deposits client> #>  deposits service : zenodo #>            sandbox: TRUE #>          url_base : https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/ #>  Current deposits : <none> #> #>    hostdata : <none> #>    metadata : 3 terms (see 'metadata' element for details) cli <- depositsClient$new (service = "zenodo", sandbox = TRUE) cli$deposit_fill_metadata (metadata)

A universal client for depositing and accessing research data

(Viewing web browser require logging private sandbox deposits).

title:
A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere
text: deposit really data, just metadata. Uploading data deposit simple following function: Details files associated deposits stored data.frame held cli$hostdata$files running command include row item whose “filename” “-data.dat”. deposit_download_file() function reverse: Files default downloaded current working directory, elsewhere specified additional path parameter. deposit_download_file() function main client function returns value, case full path downloaded file.
code: cli$deposit_upload_file (path = "<path>/<to>/my-data.dat") cli$hostdata$files #>                           checksum    filename filesize #> 1 5955bb96a8fee3bc89549bde9ef9b470 my-data.dat      829 #>                                     id #> 1 618ae9b9-af48-4b86-aa37-7b4e767dccb7 #>                                                 links.download #> 1 https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/files/<file-hash>/my-data.dat #>                                                                links.self #> 1 https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api/deposit/depositions/1065666/files/<hash> path <- cli$deposit_download_file ("my-data.dat")

title:
A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere
text: deposits client offers functions beyond demonstrated . command ls(cli) lists elements R6 client, including variables functions, functions prefixed deposit_ deposits_. Additional functions include: deposit_delete(deposit_id) delete nominated deposit; deposit_retrieve(deposit_id) populate local deposits client information specified deposit; deposit_update(deposit_id) update deposit server local metadata; deposits_list() update lists current deposits within client. deposits_search() search deposits online service. See introductory vignette details.

title:
A universal client for depositing and accessing research data
    anywhere

code: depositsClient$deposits_search(   search_string = NULL,   page_size = 10L,   page_number = 1L,   ... )

title: depositsClient — depositsClient
text: search_string Single string search page_size Number records return one page page_number Starting page return results; used combination 'page_size' pagination. ... Named pairs query parameters. Zenodo parameters described https://developers.zenodo.org/#list36, currently include: status: either "draft" "published" sort: either "bestmatch" (default) "mostrecent" all_versions: Either "true" "false" communities: Search deposits within specified communities type: Return deposits specified type subtype: Return deposits specified subtype bound: geolocation bounding box custom: Custom keywords Figshare parameters described https://docs.figshare.com/#articles_search, currently include: resource_doi: return deposits matching 'resource_doi' item_type: Return deopsits specified type (integer). doi: return deposits matching DOI handle: return deposits matching handle project_id: return deposits within specified project order: Order sorting results; one "published_date", "modified_date", "views", "shares", "downloads", "cites" search_for: Search term. order_direction: "asc" "desc" institution: return deposits specified institution (integer) group: return deposits specified group (integer) published_since: return deposits published since specified date (YYYY-MM-DD) modified_since: return deposits modified since specified date (YYYY-MM-DD)

title: depositsClient — depositsClient
text: data.frame data deposits matching search parameters (format depending deposits service.)

code: if (FALSE) { # make a client cli <- depositsClient$new ("zenodo") # or: cli <- depositsClient$new ("figshare") print (cli)  # methods cli$deposits_list ()  # Fill depositsClient metadata metadata <- list (     title = "New Title",     abstract = "This is the abstract",     creator = list ("A. Person", "B. Person") ) cli$deposit_fill_metadata (metadata) print (cli)  # or pass metadata directly at construction of new client cli <- depositsClient$new ("figshare", metadata = metadata) }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `depositsClient$deposits_search` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { cli <- depositsClient$new (service = "figshare") search_results <- cli$deposits_search (search_string = "Text string query", page_size = 5L) }

what: Read a metadata yaml file and convert to atom4R DCMI object. — deposits_meta_to_dcmi
title: Read a metadata yaml file and convert to atom4R DCMI object. — deposits_meta_to_dcmi
text: Metadata templates can generated deposits_metadata_template, values manually entered. functions loads completed template converts DCEntry object atom4R package.

title: Read a metadata yaml file and convert to atom4R DCMI object. — deposits_meta_to_dcmi
code: deposits_meta_to_dcmi(filename = NULL, id = "my-id")

title: Read a metadata yaml file and convert to atom4R DCMI object. — deposits_meta_to_dcmi
text: filename Name completed metadata file generated deposits_metadata_template. id unique identifier deposit, single character string.

title: Read a metadata yaml file and convert to atom4R DCMI object. — deposits_meta_to_dcmi
code: filename <- tempfile (fileext = ".json") deposits_metadata_template (filename) m <- readLines (filename) m [grep ("Title", m)] <- "  \"Title\": \"New Title\"" m [grep ("Type", m)] <- "  \"Type\": \"Software\"" m [grep ("Description", m)] <-     "  \"Description\": \"Description of software\"" # Values can be entered in JSON format: m [grep ("TableOfContents", m)] <-     "  \"TableOfContents\": {\"one\": \"First\", \"two\": \"Second\"}" dc <- deposits_meta_to_dcmi (filename, id = "my-id")

text: fields defined Dublin Core Metadata Initiative (DCMI), defined https://www.dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/dcmi-terms/, extracted atom4R package. template produced function json format can manually edited provide metadata deposit.

title: Write an empty metadata template to local file — deposits_metadata_template
code: deposits_metadata_template(filename = NULL, metadata = NULL)

title: Write an empty metadata template to local file — deposits_metadata_template
text: filename Name full path local file template written. file created. file name already exists, must first deleted. metadata named list DCMI metadata, names match dcmi_terms, entries generally single character values. Multiple entries generally permitted, example multiple authors can specified multiple list items named "Creator", specifies one author.

title: Write an empty metadata template to local file — deposits_metadata_template
